===''Pokémon the Series''=== ====''[[Pokémon the Series: Diamond and Pearl]]''==== [[File:Volkner DP anime.png|thumb|250px|Volkner in ''[[Pokémon the Series: Diamond and Pearl]]'']] Volkner grew up battling gangs and [[Pokémon hunter|Pokémon poachers]] on the streets of [[Sunyshore City]]. He came across [[Flint]] and decided to challenge him to a {{pkmn|battle}}, though he lost the match. Volkner challenged Flint sometime later and won, thus igniting an intense rivalry. The two later became friends after battling against the [[Proprietor]] in a Tag Battle. When Flint turned 20 he left Sunyshore to pursue his own journey, and eventually became a member of the [[Sinnoh]] [[Elite Four]], while Volkner assumed the role of Sunyshore City's [[Gym Leader]]. After becoming the Gym Leader, Volkner became more interested in technology and he distanced himself from battling. The obsession led him to design the automated walkways of Sunyshore, the installation of solar panels, and the city's famous [[Sunyshore Tower]]. In ''[[DP141|Challenging a Towering Figure!]]'', Volkner was first mentioned by [[Palmer]], who said that, after seeing his battling skills, {{Ash}} would be able to give Volkner "a shock". In ''[[DP163|Fighting Ire with Fire!]]'', [[Paul]] also hinted that something was amiss at the [[Sunyshore Gym]], angrily denouncing it as "not legit". In ''[[DP165|Flint Sparks the Fire!]]'', when Ash arrived at the Sunyshore Gym, he was surprised that Volkner had installed a machine at the entrance that was simply giving away free {{Badge|Beacon}}s to {{pkmn|Trainer}}s. Ash was determined to have a genuine [[Gym]] {{pkmn|battle}} so he began knocking on the door, only to be taken off the property by its robotic security. Flint noticed this and escorted Ash and {{ashfr}} to Volkner's location at the Sunyshore Tower. Ash requested a battle from Volkner, but he declined and stated that he was bored. Later, Volkner walked into the Proprietor's café and quickly began arguing with Flint. The Proprietor then suggested that Flint battle Ash instead, and requested that Volkner watch the match. After witnessing Ash's battle with Flint, he became interested in battling again and decided to accept Ash's challenge for a Gym battle. [[File:Young Volkner.png|thumb|left|250px|Young Volkner with his {{p|Pikachu}}]] In ''[[DP166|The Fleeing Tower of Sunyshore!]]'', Volkner and Ash began their Gym battle. However, it was quickly interrupted as {{TRT}} stole the Sunyshore Tower, causing a citywide blackout. After Ash defeated Team Rocket with the help of his newly [[Evolution|evolved]] {{AP|Torterra}}, Volkner returned the tower back to its original place. However, as both the tower and Gym were badly damaged, Volkner told Ash that he had to repair them before they could have a [[rematch]]. Ash agreed to the terms and said that he would use the extra time for more training. In ''[[DP177|A Grand Fight for Winning!]]'', Volkner sends a message to [[Nurse Joy]] to let Ash know that he was ready to do battle with him. In ''[[DP179|The Eighth Wonder of the Sinnoh World!]]'', Ash returned to Sunyshore to be the first to challenge Volkner since Team Rocket stole the Sunyshore Tower. During the last round between {{AP|Infernape}} and {{p|Luxray}}, Infernape's {{a|Blaze}} [[Ability]] was activated once again. This time, however, Infernape was able to take control of his [[Ability]], and by using the power that his {{type|Fire}} attacks had gained, he won the match. This earned Ash the Beacon Badge, thus allowing him to enter the [[Sinnoh League]]. In ''[[DP180|Four Roads Diverged in a Pokémon Port!]]'', Flint thanks Ash that it is because of him that Volkner has returned to being the Trainer he was before and has now returned to normal Gym activities. Volkner made a cameo appearance in the ending credits of ''[[M13|Zoroark: Master of Illusions]]''. ====''[[Pokémon Journeys: The Series]]''==== [[File:Volkner Journeys.png|thumb|250px|Volkner in ''[[Pokémon Journeys: The Series]]'']] Volkner's silhouette was seen at the end of ''[[JN065|Thrash of the Titans!]]'', where it was revealed he had entered the [[World Coronation Series]]. In ''[[JN076|All Out, All of the Time!]]'', it was revealed that Ash's next battle in the World Coronation Series was to be against Volkner, who was currently ranked 27th in the competition. Volkner reappeared in ''[[JN077|Ultra Exciting from the Shocking Start!]]'', where he had his World Coronation Series battle against Ash at the Sunyshore Gym. After a tough battle, Ash was able to defeat Volkner again and climb higher in the Ultra Class. Volkner predicted that Ash would become "the eye of the storm" in not only the Ultra Class, but even in the Master Class, where {{an|Cynthia}} was ranked. In ''[[JN117|The Fiery Road to Mastership!]]'', Volkner appeared watching the [[Masters Eight Tournament]] match between Cynthia and {{an|Iris}} alongside Flint on a [[Rotom Phone]]. ====Character==== [[File:Ignitionshipping.png|thumb|left|250px|Volkner and Flint]] Volkner is renowned for being "electrifying" and nicknamed the "Shining Shocking Star of {{ci|Sunyshore}}" because of his enthusiasm and technique in battle. He utilizes offense [[move]]s as a defensive strategy to counter an opponent's efforts. Volkner aims to attack his opponent's Pokémon at close range to ensure the most damage. He is regarded as bright and intelligent, though he is prone to becoming obsessed with his interests and tires of them when things become predictable. However, after becoming the [[Sunyshore Gym|Sunyshore]] [[Gym Leader]] and experiencing numerous weak challengers, Volkner completely lost any interest in battling. Furthermore, Volkner became completely aloof and somewhat cold, always having an empty look on his face, and always speaking in a bored tone. His aloofness even went as far as boringly telling [[Flint]] to punch him if he felt like it after being threatened by the former, and Volkner's disinterest seemed to have extended towards his friends, as Volkner seemed to have gained some level of resentment towards Flint, once sarcastically referring to him as "Mr. [[Elite Four]]", possibly from the fact that Flint became able to travel whilst Volkner remained in Sunyshore City, surrounded by weak challengers. After witnessing {{Ash}} putting himself in harm's way to protect his {{AP|Pikachu}}, Volkner seemed to have finally snapped out of his disinterest, as Ash's actions reminded him of his own protective actions in his childhood regarding his Pikachu, and seeing Ash's determination, as well as hearing the [[Proprietor]]'s speech about Volkner's determination causing him to abandon his ways as a [[Pokémon hunter|poacher]], finally caused Volkner to reignite his battling spirit. Volkner also returned to his previously cheerful and confident self. Since Ash's challenge in ''[[DP179|The Eighth Wonder of the Sinnoh World!]]'', Flint reported that news of Volkner's return to battling spread quickly throughout the region, as countless potential challenger's were queuing to go up against him, although Volkner declined all of them, keeping his promise to have his first official return match against Ash. He holds deep respect and immense sentiments for his hometown of Sunyshore. His technology projects and massive construction efforts were designed to highlight Sunyshore's best assets and separate it from its previously grimy and criminal gang history. ====Pokémon==== As expected of an {{type|Electric}} Trainer, Volkner has a team made up of several extremely powerful Electric Pokémon. In Gym battles, Volkner uses super effective moves to defeat his opponents, however during his battle in the [[World Coronation Series]] Volkner gave up several resources and strategies such as using {{m|Electric Terrain}} to increase his Pokémon's attacks and using moves like {{m|Hex}} after {{status|Paralysis|paralyzing}} his opponents. In both battles against Ash, Volkner was able to force Ash to the max and use his Pokémon's special [[Ability|Abilities]] and [[Z-Move]]s. Despite being his oldest partner, Volkner uses his {{p|Raichu}} very little in battle, appearing only to help against the problems caused by {{TRT}}. =====On hand===== {{MissingInfo|2|enva|java}} This listing is of Volkner's known {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} in {{aniseries|PTS}}: {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Volkner |pkmn=Raichu |gender=male |stage1=Pikachu |type1=Electric |img=Volkner Pikachu.png |cap=Volkner's Pikachu |img2=Volkner Raichu.png |cap2=Volkner's Raichu |epnum=DP165 |epname=Flint Sparks the Fire! |vajp=Satsuki Yukino |vajpnote=as Pikachu |vajp2=Chie Satō |vajp2note=as Raichu |vaen=Satsuki Yukino |vaennote=as Pikachu |vaen2=Chie Satō |vaen2note=as Raichu |desc={{p|Raichu}} is Volkner's very first Pokémon. He first appeared in ''[[DP165|Flint Sparks the Fire!]]'', where he reunited with [[Flint]], and in the [[DP166|next episode]]. Raichu has been shown to be {{pkmn2|Walking|outside of his Poké Ball}} most of the time. Volkner first received his Raichu as a {{p|Pikachu}} when he was young. He battled with [[Flint's Chimchar]] and lost. However, in their [[rematch]], Pikachu defeated {{p|Chimchar}} with {{m|Iron Tail}}. Since then, Flint and Volkner became friends and rivals. Later, Pikachu was battling the [[Proprietor]]'s {{p|Houndoom}}, when the latter was a [[Pokémon hunter|Pokémon poacher]] at the time. Houndoom dealt some damage to Chimchar and Pikachu, but Chimchar wasn't able to continue, leaving Pikachu to do it on its own. Volkner was very determined to battle the Proprietor. After a long battle, the Proprietor was defeated and became friends with Volkner and Flint. Pikachu later [[Evolution|evolved]] into a Raichu prior to Volkner becoming the [[Sunyshore City]] [[Gym Leader]]. Raichu was very strong as he defeated several Pokémon such as a {{pkmn|Trainer}}'s {{p|Weavile}} and {{p|Mothim}} over time. However, Volkner did not select Raichu in any of his matches against {{Ash}}. Raichu briefly appeared alongside Volkner during the ending credits of ''[[M13|Zoroark: Master of Illusions]]''. Raichu's known moves are {{m|Iron Tail}}, {{m|Thunderbolt}}, {{m|Volt Tackle}}, and {{m|Thunder}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Volkner |pkmn=Luxray |type1=Electric |gender=male |img=Volkner Luxray.png |epnum=DP166 |epname=The Fleeing Tower of Sunyshore! |vajp=Katsuyuki Konishi |vajpnote=[[DP166]]-[[DP179]] |vajp2=Kensuke Satō |vajp2note=[[JN077]] |vaen=Sarah Natochenny |desc=Volkner's {{p|Luxray}} appeared for the first time in ''[[DP166|The Fleeing Tower of Sunyshore!]]'', where he {{pkmn|battle}}d [[Ash's Grotle]] during {{Ash}}'s [[Gym]] challenge. However, the battle was called off due to {{TRT}} terrorizing the city. During the [[rematch]], Luxray first battled with {{AP|Pikachu}}, and won after taking hits from several {{m|Iron Tail}}s. Ash next used {{AP|Infernape}}, which got badly injured during the battle. Due to this, his [[Ability]] {{a|Blaze}} got activated. However, this time {{p|Infernape}} was able to control his Ability, and with the powers gained through it, the Flame Pokémon quickly defeated Luxray, serving the final blow with a devastating {{m|Flame Wheel}}, earning Ash the {{Badge|Beacon}}. Luxray was seen in a flashback in ''[[DP183|League Unleashed!]]''. Luxray reappeared in ''[[JN077|Ultra Exciting from the Shocking Start!]]'', where he was the first Pokémon in Volkner's [[World Coronation Series]] match against Ash. Luxray first went up against {{AP|Lucario}} and {{AP|Gengar}} before being recalled. He later battled Lucario once more, with the battle ending in a tie. Luxray's known moves are {{m|Spark}}, {{m|Thunder}}, {{m|Shock Wave}}, {{m|Thunder Fang}}, {{m|Double Team}}, {{m|Iron Tail}}, {{m|Electric Terrain}}, {{m|Rising Voltage}}, and {{m|Snarl}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Volkner |pkmn=Electivire |type1=Electric |img=Volkner Electivire.png |epnum=DP179 |epname=The Eighth Wonder of the Sinnoh World! |vajp=Yūichi Nakamura |vajpnote=[[JN077]] |vaen=Bill Rogers |desc={{p|Electivire}} was the first Pokémon which Volkner used during his [[rematch]] against {{Ash}}. Ash first used {{AP|Torterra}}, but it was easily defeated. Next Ash chose to use {{p|Pikachu}}. With his {{m|Thunderbolt}}, Pikachu triggered Electivire's [[Ability]], making it an even harder target to hit. However, when one of Electivire's punches connected with Pikachu, the Thunderbolt Pokémon was {{status|Paralysis|paralyzed}} by Pikachu's {{a|Static}}. After this, Pikachu was able to finish Electivire with a well-aimed {{m|Iron Tail}}. Electivire reappeared in ''[[JN077|Ultra Exciting from the Shocking Start!]]'', when it was the third Pokémon in his [[World Coronation Series]] match against Ash. It was used to negate Pikachu's Thunderbolt with its Motor Drive Ability. After Pikachu, it briefly battled Ash's Lucario before being recalled. Electivire later battled Ash's Pikachu again, but it was eventually defeated by Pikachu's {{m|10,000,000 Volt Thunderbolt}}, which proved to be too much for Motor Drive to absorb. In ''[[JN117|The Fiery Road to Mastership!]]'', Electivire was shown watching {{an|Cynthia}}'s [[Masters Eight Tournament]] match against {{an|Iris}} alongside its Trainer. Electivire's known moves are {{m|Fire Punch}}, {{m|Ice Punch}}, {{m|Iron Tail}}, {{m|Wild Charge}}, and {{m|Thunder Punch}}, and its Ability is {{a|Motor Drive}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Volkner |pkmn=Jolteon |type1=Electric |img=Volkner Jolteon.png |epnum=DP179 |epname=The Eighth Wonder of the Sinnoh World! |vajp=Kiyotaka Furushima |vaen=Kayzie Rogers |desc={{p|Jolteon}} was the second Pokémon used by Volkner during his and {{Ash}}'s [[rematch]]. It has impressive agility and was shown to be able to use consecutive Shadow Ball attacks. Ash chose to use his {{AP|Infernape}}, which soon defeated the Lightning Pokémon with a {{m|Mach Punch}}. Jolteon's known moves are {{m|Thunderbolt}} and {{m|Shadow Ball}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Volkner |pkmn=Rotom |type1=Electric |type2=Flying |img=Volkner Fan Rotom.png |epnum=JN077 |epname=Ultra Exciting from the Shocking Start! |vajp= |vaen= |desc={{p|Rotom}} was the second Pokémon used by Volkner in his [[World Coronation Series]] match against Ash. After using {{m|Thunder Wave}} on {{AP|Gengar}}, Rotom was able to defeat it with {{m|Hex}}. Rotom was recalled when {{AP|Pikachu}} trapped it with {{m|Electroweb}} and was about to use {{m|Thunderbolt}} on it. Later, Rotom battled against Lucario, weakening it with Hex after it was paralyzed, only to be defeated by a {{m|Reversal}}. Rotom's known moves are {{m|Air Slash}}, {{m|Thunder Wave}}, and {{m|Hex}}.}} =====Traveling with===== {| width="100%" |- | {{ActivePoké|||nick=Rotom Phone|Ignitionshipping.png|electric|ghost|link=Rotom Phone}} | | |} ====Achievements==== [[File:Volkner Profile WCS.png|thumb|250px|Volkner's profile in the [[World Coronation Series]]]] =====Pokémon competitions===== ======World Coronation Series====== Volkner has competed in the [[World Coronation Series]].
